ProjectRevision.title: ProjectRevision
ProjectRevision.description:
One complete immutable project input captured by a `prepare` request. Site
bytes are carried here even though only their normalized page listing
affects semantic compilation.

ClosedSiteBuild.description:
Proof that a [`SiteBuild`]'s render-plan closure is fully materialized. This
wrapper is the value an external builder should accept when callbacks are not
part of its architecture.

SchemaVersion.description:
Wire-format discriminator. A new incompatible contract requires a new enum
variant rather than silently changing existing hashing semantics.

BuildId.description: Deterministic identity of the complete SiteBuild document (except this field).

revision.description:
An SCM revision when available; otherwise a caller-defined immutable
revision label. Exact source hashes remain authoritative.

SourceManifest.description: Exact source bytes that define a project revision.

ContentRef.description: Exact immutable-content reference shared by compilers, renderers, and hosts.

sha256.description: The algorithm is fixed to SHA-256 and encoded in the field name.

PackageLock.description:
Exact package closure. Keys are repeated in each value so a key/value mismatch
cannot be hidden by a serialization adapter.

content.description:
Exact deterministic prepared-package carrier. This content-addressed
value binds the complete package representation consumed by execution,
not merely its registry name/version or a normalized semantic subset.

RenderPlan.description:
Artifacts a renderer promises it can consume without discovering undeclared
requirements. Sealing also follows their typed artifact read dependencies,
so this set contains roots rather than requiring callers to duplicate the
transitive closure manually.

ArtifactKey.description: Typed identity of one semantic or rendered artifact.

FragmentScope.description:
The subject of a generated fragment. Some Publisher includes describe the
guide as a whole (for example dependency/summary material) rather than one
resource, so resource scope must not be fabricated for them.

SourcePath.description: Canonical project-relative path. Paths are POSIX-style even on native hosts.

AssetNamespace.description:
Asset ownership is part of identity. Identical relative paths from a template,
authored input, Publisher runtime, and generator are distinct artifacts until
an explicit assembly policy chooses which one is served.

ArtifactCatalog.description:
Sorted typed artifact catalog. The wire shape is a list because complex enum
keys cannot be represented faithfully as JSON object property names.

ArtifactState.description:
State is exhaustive on purpose. There is no absent/null state that a consumer
could accidentally interpret as either "not requested" or "failed".

sequence.description:
Stable producer-assigned emission order. Keeping it in the value preserves
meaningful diagnostic order while making collection insertion irrelevant.

line.description: One-based, matching SUSHI's source line reporting.

column.description: Zero-based, matching the parser/ANTLR column convention.

operation.description:
Functional operation when this observation belongs to an immutable
Build. Lifecycle/package/project-source events omit it.

buildId.description:
Immutable build identity when one exists. Acquisition events that occur
before prepare establishes an identity omit it.

phase.description:
Stable machine-readable phase within the operation. `message` remains
presentation text and must not be parsed by benchmarks.

source.description:
Clock domain which measured this event. Browser hosts align Window and
Worker clocks through `performance.timeOrigin`; native hosts may omit it.

startMs.description:
Unix-epoch milliseconds at the beginning of this measured span. This is
observational only and never participates in build identity.

ResolutionStep.title: ResolutionStep
ResolutionStep.description:
One iteration's answer: the two well-defined sets, what is still missing, and
whether the closure is complete.

resolver_schema.description:
Schema for the resolver semantics represented by this result. Hosts may
persist an exact closure as an optimization, but must discard it when
this value changes and must still re-run this resolver before use.

compile_set.description:
Stock-SUSHI COMPILE load set (non-transitive), in stock load order. Every
entry is a concrete `pkg#ver` the compile needs mounted.

context_closure.description:
Transitive snapshot/render context closure rooted at the exact compile
set and walked over the MOUNTED packages (R4-compat filtered,
core-canonicalized), with core first then discovery order.

resolution_support.description:
Mounted package manifests read while proving `context_closure`, including
packages later excluded by compatibility rules. Replaying a cached
resolution must mount these witnesses too; the final closure alone is
not sufficient to prove that an excluded dependency remains excluded.

missing.description:
Packages referenced by either set that are not yet mounted (or whose version
could not be resolved). Fetch these, mount, and resolve again.

satisfied.description:
True iff nothing is missing — the mounted set fully satisfies both closures
and the host loop has reached its fixpoint.

mutable_requests.description:
Every non-concrete version request which influenced this step. A cached
closure is not fresh merely because its concrete packages still exist:
the host must refresh the candidate universe for these requests before
asking Rust to verify the closure again.

PackageRequest.description:
Ordered package request produced from a project's SUSHI dependency rules.

This is the acquisition-side counterpart to `PackageStore::for_project`: it
preserves stock SUSHI's load order, but leaves mutable coordinates such as
`latest`, `current`, and `dev` unresolved so the acquisition layer can resolve
them against registries/CAS and record a lock.

MissingPackage.description: One resolvable package coordinate the host must acquire + mount.

version.description:
The concrete version the resolver selected (if it could resolve one), or the
raw requested coordinate when it could not (`latest`/`current` with no index).

reason.description: Why this package is in `missing`: the exact, host-renderable reason.

set.description: The set that referenced it (`compile` or `context`), for the host's UI.

MissingReason.description: The precise reason a package could not be satisfied from the mounted set.

oneOf.description:
The coordinate is fully resolved (concrete version) but not yet mounted —
the host should fetch + mount it, then resolve again.

The request needs version resolution (`latest`/`current`/`M.N.x`) and no
host [`VersionIndex`] entry (or no matching version) was available. The host
must supply an index (registry query) and resolve again. NEVER a guess.

RequestedSet.description: Which resolution set surfaced a missing package.

oneOf.description: Needed for the SUSHI compile load (configured/auto deps + core).
Needed for the transitive snapshot/render context closure.

MutableVersionRequest.description:
A version request whose answer can change without the project config
changing (`latest`, `current`, `dev`, `x`, or `*`).

VersionIndex.title: VersionIndex
VersionIndex.description:
A host-supplied index of the versions available for a package id, used to
resolve `latest` / `current` / `M.N.x` requests without the resolver doing any
I/O of its own (data in, decisions in Rust). The host populates this from
whatever it can see — a registry manifest, the set of cached/mounted dirs, a
committed lockfile — and the resolver picks the winning version by the SAME
rule stock SUSHI/FPL use (highest semver, release over pre-release).

Absent an entry for a package whose request needs resolution, the resolver
reports a precise `missing` reason rather than guessing.

versions.description:
package id -> the list of available version strings (order irrelevant; the
resolver sorts).

PackageMountResult.title: PackageMountResult
PackageMountResult.description:
Runtime footprint and lazy-inflate counters for all compact prepared layers.
Backings shared by several package layers are counted exactly once.

ContentRef.title: ContentRef

ContentRef.description: Exact immutable-content reference shared by compilers, renderers, and hosts.

sha256.description: The algorithm is fixed to SHA-256 and encoded in the field name.

recipeSha256.description:
Digest of the exact renderer code/assets/toolchain recipe used for this
invocation. Human version labels alone are not cache-safe.
